name: Pilgrim Baptist
description_short: Pilgrim is an Indian beauty and personal care e-commerce retailer selling skincare, haircare, makeup, and fragrance products—not a consumer finance company.
description_long:
Pilgrim is a direct-to-consumer beauty brand based in India that operates an e-commerce platform at discoverpilgrim.com. The company sells skincare serums, moisturizers, shampoos, hair growth products, makeup items, and fragrances to Indian consumers. Their product lineup focuses on dermatologically-tested formulations with active ingredients like Vitamin C, Niacinamide, Retinol, and plant-based extracts.

The company offers tiered discounts based on cart value (20% off ₹499+, 30% off ₹999+, 40% off ₹1499+) and runs promotional campaigns like voting contests to reduce product prices and referral programs offering discounts down to ₹5. Their bestselling products include a 10% Vitamin C Face Serum, Redensyl Hair Growth Serum, and a Korean Hydra Glow Moisturizer. The website displays extensive customer reviews on products.

Pilgrim differentiates itself through in-vivo testing claims, Korean beauty product collaborations, and aggressive pricing strategies tied to community engagement. Their marketing emphasizes rapid results ("Glowing Skin in 5 Days," "New Hair Growth in 28 Days") and positions products as solutions for specific skin concerns like pigmentation, dandruff, and damage repair.

This company is fundamentally a beauty product retailer with no connection to consumer finance, lending, credit services, or financial products. The categorization as "payday-alternatives" appears to be a database error, as Pilgrim operates exclusively in the beauty and personal care sector.
services: Vitamin C and Niacinamide face serums
Hair growth serums with Redensyl and Anagain
Hair masks and damage repair treatments
Tiered discount promotions based on cart value
Email newsletter for early access sales
Product voting contests with price reduction mechanics
pros: Wide product range spanning skincare, haircare, makeup, and fragrance in one platform
Aggressive tiered discount structure incentivizing larger purchases (up to 40% off)
Community-driven pricing model with voting contests and referral programs
Extensive customer reviews and ratings visible for transparency
Claims of in-vivo testing and dermatological formulation standards
Multiple product sizes available for popular items allowing flexibility
cons: No financial services, lending, or credit products offered—completely miscategorized in CreditDoc
All prices displayed in Indian Rupees (₹), limiting accessibility to Indian consumers only
Marketing claims like '5 Days' and '28 Days' for results are unverified and potentially overstated
No information visible about ingredient sourcing, manufacturing standards, or regulatory certifications
best_for: Lower fees and better loan rates than banks
diagnosis: Pilgrim is an Indian beauty and personal care e-commerce retailer with zero connection to consumer finance, lending, credit services, or payday alternatives. This company should be removed from CreditDoc entirely as it operates in an entirely different industry and provides no financial products or services whatsoever.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
